title: What is HTML?
theory:
What is HTML?

HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language. It is the standard language used to create web pages. HTML describes the structure of a web page using a series of elements called tags.

Think of HTML as the skeleton of a webpage — it gives structure to the content you see in your browser. Every website you visit is built with HTML at its core.

How does HTML work?

HTML documents are text files that your browser interprets and displays as a visual webpage. The browser reads the HTML tags and renders the content accordingly.

The most basic HTML document looks like this example below. Try clicking Try It in Editor to experiment!
codeExample:
Hello, World!

This is my first HTML page.

challenge.instruction: Create a paragraph that says 'I am learning HTML!' inside the body tag.

quiz.question: What is the role of HTML in a webpage?
options: It styles the webpage with colors
It provides the structure/skeleton of the webpage
